topics.label: Metrics
topics.description: Time-series metrics collection, aggregation, DogStatsD, custom metrics, metric types, and tagging.

studyPointers.label: Metric Types (gauge, count, rate, histogram, distribution)

topics.label: Log Management
topics.description: Log collection, pipelines, processors, indexes, archives, and live tail.

examScenarios.title: HTTP 500s appear in raw app logs
examScenarios.prompt: A training app starts returning intermittent 500 responses. The team can see the messages in raw logs, but wants to search by status code and service name during the exam scenario.
examScenarios.focus: Recognize that parsing and remapping structured attributes during ingestion makes logs searchable and groupable.

topics.label: APM & Tracing
topics.description: Application Performance Monitoring, distributed tracing, spans, service maps, and profiling.

examScenarios.title: Frontend request triggers a slow backend endpoint
examScenarios.prompt: Users report that clicking a button feels slow. You need to identify the end-to-end request path across the web tier and backend service in an exam-style troubleshooting prompt.
examScenarios.focus: Identify traces as the full request path and spans as the individual operations that explain where latency was spent.

topics.label: Dashboards & Notebooks
topics.description: Dashboard creation, widget types, template variables, timeboards, screenboards, and notebooks.

topics.label: Monitors & Alerting
topics.description: Monitor types, alert conditions, notifications, downtimes, SLOs, and composite monitors.

topics.label: Agent & Integrations
topics.description: Datadog Agent installation, configuration, checks, integrations, and autodiscovery.

examScenarios.title: Logs never arrive from a host
examScenarios.prompt: An Agent is installed and infrastructure metrics appear, but application logs never show up. The exam asks what configuration setting should be checked first.
examScenarios.focus: Remember which Agent features are enabled separately and which files control host-wide versus integration-specific behavior.

topics.label: Real User Monitoring (RUM)
topics.description: Browser and mobile RUM, session replay, Core Web Vitals, error tracking, and user actions.

examScenarios.title: Button click fails in the browser
examScenarios.prompt: A user clicks a button in a single-page app and sees an error. The exam prompt asks which product would capture the user action, the browser error, and the affected view.
examScenarios.focus: Map browser-side actions, errors, resources, and views to RUM rather than to traces or infrastructure metrics.

topics.label: Synthetic Monitoring
topics.description: Browser tests, API tests, multistep API tests, test coverage, and private locations.

topics.label: Tagging
topics.description: Unified service tagging, tag best practices, reserved tags, and tag-based filtering.

examScenarios.title: Metrics and traces disagree on the environment name
examScenarios.prompt: A service shows `env:prod` in one place and `env:production` in another. The exam asks why correlation breaks between telemetry types.
examScenarios.focus: Know that Unified Service Tagging depends on consistent `env`, `service`, and `version` values across telemetry.

topics.label: Infrastructure Monitoring
topics.description: Host maps, container monitoring, process monitoring, network performance monitoring.

examScenarios.title: A host is healthy but one process is saturating CPU
examScenarios.prompt: A host tile looks busy and the exam asks which Datadog infrastructure view helps you move from host health to the exact process consuming resources.
examScenarios.focus: Differentiate between Host Map or Infrastructure List for broad visibility and Live Processes for process-level troubleshooting.

topics.label: Service Catalog
topics.description: Service definitions, ownership, metadata, and service-level insights.

topics.label: Security Monitoring
topics.description: Cloud Security Management, CSPM, SIEM, detection rules, and signals.
